info.description:
In order to use the Trucker Search API please first contact us to get an API Token. This token needs to be sent as a HTTP header argument in every request to our API.

api-token=“SECRET KEY”

Please keep your api-token a secret. If the token is compromised please contact us to get a new one.

This api token authenticates your application and gives it access to the API.

The API token should not be confused with the end-user-token. That token is given to you via the end_user_authentications endpoint when the user logs in. This token is needed in order to use certain features, and get certain sensitive information.

Both Tokens are stateless, and delivered via the HTTP headers.

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"io/ioutil"
)

func main() {

	url := "https://www.truckersearch.com/v1/alerts"

	req, _ := http.NewRequest("X-RESTLET", url, nil)

	req.Header.Add("Accept", "application/json")
	req.Header.Add("Content-Type", "application/json")
	req.Header.Add("api-token", "REPLACE_KEY_VALUE")

	res, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)

	defer res.Body.Close()
	body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(res.Body)

	fmt.Println(res)
	fmt.Println(string(body))

}

HttpResponse<String> response = Unirest.customMethod("X-RESTLET","https://www.truckersearch.com/v1/alerts")

  .header("Content-Type", "application/json")
  .header("api-token", "REPLACE_KEY_VALUE")
  .asString();

require 'uri'
require 'net/http'

class Net::HTTP::X-restlet < Net::HTTPRequest
  METHOD = 'X-RESTLET'
  REQUEST_HAS_BODY = 'false'
  RESPONSE_HAS_BODY = true
end

url = URI("https://www.truckersearch.com/v1/alerts")

http = Net::HTTP.new(url.host, url.port)
http.use_ssl = true
http.verify_mode = OpenSSL::SSL::VERIFY_NONE

request = Net::HTTP::X-restlet.new(url)
request["Accept"] = 'application/json'
request["Content-Type"] = 'application/json'
request["api-token"] = 'REPLACE_KEY_VALUE'

response = http.request(request)
puts response.read_body

curl --request X-RESTLET \
  --url https://www.truckersearch.com/v1/alerts \
  --header 'Accept: application/json' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header 'api-token: REPLACE_KEY_VALUE'

http X-RESTLET https://www.truckersearch.com/v1/alerts \
  Accept:application/json \
  Content-Type:application/json \
  api-token:REPLACE_KEY_VALUE

wget --verbose \
  --method X-RESTLET \
  --header 'Accept: application/json' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header 'api-token: REPLACE_KEY_VALUE' \
  --output-document \
  - https://www.truckersearch.com/v1/alerts

	req.Header.Add("Accept", "application/json")

	req.Header.Add("end-user-token", "NO DEFAULT")

	res, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)

	defer res.Body.Close()
	body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(res.Body)

	fmt.Println(res)
	fmt.Println(string(body))

}

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"
	"net/http"
	"io/ioutil"
)

func main() {

	url := "https://www.truckersearch.com/v1/end_user_authentications"

	payload := strings.NewReader("{\"login\":\"<ADD EXAMPLE VALUE>\",\"realm\":\"<ADD EXAMPLE VALUE>\",\"secret\":\"<ADD EXAMPLE VALUE>\"}")

	req, _ := http.NewRequest("POST", url, payload)

	req.Header.Add("Accept", "application/json")
	req.Header.Add("Content-Type", "application/json")
	req.Header.Add("api-token", "REPLACE_KEY_VALUE")

	res, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)

	defer res.Body.Close()
	body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(res.Body)

	fmt.Println(res)
	fmt.Println(string(body))

}

post.description: This will send a message to any other user. Currently there are no blocks or set restrictions if an API user just started guessing. In order to get a contact_id a contact user would need to pull that info from a resume, or a job ad.

contact_id.description: Who the message is being sent to.

message.description: This can contain a small list of html tags.

get.description: This method provides the information nedeed to display all the contacts within a folder.

conversation_status.description: Sent means that the contact sent your user a message, but it has not yet been read by them. Messages are automatically marked as read when the GET /messages endpoint is called for that conversation. This will return null when the contact has not yet sent a message back to your user.

last_received.description: If the contact has sent your logged in user a message, then the newest message's sent date will appear, otherwise it will be null.

get.summary: Get a list of contacts for that folder.

get.description:
This is a coneniene method provided for pages like the driver/recruiter dasboard. The same information could be obtained by getting the folder list, and adding the new count. 

It can also be used to update individual contacts new count. An example of this is the page automatically checking for new messages every once in a while, on a per user basis.

parameters.description: Just return a count of new messages from one contact.
parameters.name: contact_id

get.summary: Returns how many unread messages the user has.

post.description: This method allows a user to add custom folders. These folders must have unique names.

schema.description:
You can use any name that has not already been taken. Certain folders are automatically created, the first time a message is sent or recieved. The names of these folders are reserved and cannot be added, modified or deleted.

Currently they are Active, Pending, Deleted, and Trash.

name.description: Name of the folder to be added.

	req.Header.Add("api-token", "REPLACE_KEY_VALUE")

only_new.description: Whether to send both new and updated resumes, or just new resumes.

send_frequency.description: Frequency in minutes that the alert is sent. We stop sending alerts between 12am and 3am CST.

last_sent.description: Last time the alert was sent.

only_new.description: Whether to send both new and updated resumes, or just new resumes.

send_frequency.description: Frequency in minutes that the alert is sent.

sender.description: Who sent the message, the logged in user, or the contact.

favorite_id.description: A subset of this users favorite resumes or job ids

contact.description: As long as 1 sub element from contact or position is filled in this will be a valid request.

position.description: As long as 1 sub element from contact or position is filled in this will be a valid request.

description.description: This can be long form text.

image_url.description: Sending in an empty string will cause us to regenerate a new image.

Image_url.description: We will download the image from the URL. If no image is found we will automatically generate one. We accept an image file of up to 1MB in size. All images will be resized to 200x200. If this value is specified image_data cannot be used.

description.description: This can be long form text.

image_data.description: Base64 string of image. Base64 data must decode into a valid jpg or png file no more than 1MB in size. All images will be resized to 200x200. If this value is specified image_url cannot be used.

locations.description:
If included the array only accepts 2 digit state codes. MO, BC, AR, and so on. 

How did Jon implement country level coding? I think at the time I said it should be done by the "front end", I am still for that, but if the hack is already in place in the backend I am open to leveraging it.

bill_method.description: Whether or not the add will automatically renew.

contact.description: The contact information for this ad.

days_left.description: A positive value indicates the ad is both inactive, and can be run for that number of days.

expires_after.description: If this date is set for today or in the future the job ad is currently active.

description.description: This is a large text field.

image_url.description: the uri path to the image.

contact.description: The contact information for this ad.

id.description: This is the id you would use to send a message to this employer. Only logged in users can see this.

description.description: This is a large text field.

states.description: Currently we return the full state name for display purposes.

state.description: Please use an Abbreviation not a full name.

id.description: This is their ID in messages.

regions.description: This is a list of possible regions. This matches the regions from searching and posting a job ad on our website. I am adding it to the API with the assumption that we can expand our resume regions to match. This should aviod some confusion and weirdness. An example would be alert options.

contact.description: The non required fields will only show up on a single resume search. You can do that by specifying the alert_id.

fax_number.description: Should we depricate this? How many new resumes come with this? Only available to subscribed users.

home_phone.description: DEVNOTE: Look into why this is 20 chars and see if we can drop it it to 11 or 13. Only available to subscribed users. We could also split this like we do with DF and have an ext section, but that doesn't make a lot of sense for the home phone.

id.description: This is the ID you would use to send a message. Only available to logged in users.

work_phone.description: Same as home phone. Only available to subscribed users.

list_order.description: Any of the results columns for a search are also valid for list_order

email.description: The email address that was used to login.

end-user-token.description: On success and end-user-token is returned.

new_account.description: Indicates if a new account was created or an existing one was used to login.

username.description: The username that was used to login.
